content.full_text:
TO: [ ] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[ ] LYNN        [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH        [ ] BOB S.
[ ] HELEN       [ ] OTHER Mary Ann; Carla

FROM: J. Pritchard
DATE: 3/13/84

[X] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: 89001; 18901

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [x] NO [ ]

CALLER Andy Ward
REPRESENTING Assemblyman Frank Vicencia
PHONE NUMBER: 57486

QUESTION: Assemblyman Vicencia sent out a questionnaire to his constituents two days before he filed his Declaration of Candidacy. Included in the questionnaire mailing was a form with a checklist of pending legislative matters, inviting people to check the items they are interested in and return the form to Assemblyman Vicencia's office. They would then be sent information on the topics they checked. Andy Ward asked if they could send a complete package of information, including all information on all the various checklist items, so that the materials sent to each person would include not only those specific items checked, but also all the other items.

ANSWER: Assemblyman Vicencia may not send a complete package of all the information, but may send only the items checked by each respondee. Section 18901(b) prohibits them sending information or materials not specifically requested.
